items.question: Do all four cases share a common sacrificial economy logic, or do preservation, glory, and extermination differ structurally?
items.dependency: All four cases require support-rating adjudication before this can be answered.

items.question: Is the enemy-as-bringer-of-death dimension present with comparable salience across all cases?
items.dependency: Lincoln pilot evidence shows weak enemy-death salience; other cases remain unrated.

items.question: Does the obligatory frame appear in all cases, or only in cases with explicit covenantal or martyrological rhetoric?
items.dependency: Per-case obligatory-frame rates require case-level adjudication.

items.question: Do absence patterns differ systematically by case type (war preservation vs. imperial vs. founding vs. genocide)?
items.dependency: Absence rows exist for all four cases but have not been cross-case adjudicated.

items.question: What is the structural role of the providence / historical destiny cluster across cases? Is it a universal feature or a historically specific one?
items.dependency: Providence appears in lincoln, am-rev, and napoleon clusters; hitler uses destiny differently. Requires comparative analysis.
